---
title: "QLoRA with torchao"
description: "Quantize the base model with torchao instead of bitsandbytes for compile-friendly, FSDP2-native LoRA/QLoRA."
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

## Background

`bitsandbytes` is the historical backend for QLoRA in Axolotl. It works well in
single-GPU and FSDP1 setups, but its 4-bit kernels graph-break under
`torch.compile` and its `Params4bit` storage needs custom patches to cooperate
with FSDP2.

[torchao](https://github.com/pytorch/ao) provides tensor-subclass quantization
(`AffineQuantizedTensor`, `NF4Tensor`) that:

- compiles cleanly with `torch.compile` (no graph breaks for INT4/INT8
  weight-only quant);
- ships native FSDP2 support via DTensor;
- shares its codebase with QAT / PTQ, so the same dtype enum (`int4`, `int8`,
  `nf4`) flows across feature.

Axolotl exposes both backends behind a single `peft:` block.

## Usage

Set `peft.backend: torchao` and pick a `weight_dtype`. The adapter type is
auto-detected from the dtype — 4-bit dtypes (`int4`, `nf4`, `nvfp4`) imply
QLoRA; sub-byte / single-byte dtypes (`int8`, `fp8`) stay as
weight-only-quantized LoRA.

```yaml
adapter: lora            # auto-promoted to qlora for int4 / nf4 / nvfp4
peft:
  backend: torchao
  weight_dtype: int4     # one of: int4, nf4, nvfp4, int8, fp8
  # group_size: 128      # defaults: int4/int8 → 128, nf4 → 64, nvfp4 → 16
```

| `weight_dtype`     | Effective adapter | torchao quant config       |
|--------------------|-------------------|----------------------------|
| `int4`             | `qlora`           | `Int4WeightOnlyConfig`     |
| `nf4`              | `qlora`           | `NF4WeightOnlyConfig`      |
| `nvfp4`            | `qlora`           | `NVFP4WeightOnlyConfig`    |
| `int8`             | `lora`            | `Int8WeightOnlyConfig`     |
| `fp8` (alias for `float8_e4m3fn`) | `lora` | `Float8WeightOnlyConfig` |

### MXFP4 (MoE only)

`mxfp4` has no weight-only torchao config for arbitrary linear layers, so
`peft.weight_dtype: mxfp4` is rejected by the loader. For Mixture-of-Experts
models, the MXFP4 LoRA path lives behind `quantize_moe_experts: true` with
`lora_target_parameters` matching the fused 3D expert tensors
(`gate_up_proj` / `down_proj`). See
[Expert Quantization](expert_quantization.qmd) for that flow.

### Mixed-quant models

`peft.backend: torchao` is a single-knob shorthand that installs *one*
`TorchAoConfig` covering every linear layer in the model. It deliberately does
*not* try to compose with other quantization mechanisms in axolotl —
combining them produces silent overrides or fights over the same tensors.
The validator and loader reject the conflicts explicitly:

| Combination                                                 | Outcome |
|-------------------------------------------------------------|---------|
| `peft.backend: torchao` + `model_quantization_config: …`    | rejected at validation |
| `peft.backend: torchao` + `quantize_moe_experts: true`      | rejected at validation |
| `peft.backend: torchao` + `gptq: true`                      | rejected at validation |
| `peft.backend: torchao` + checkpoint with embedded quant    | rejected at load time |

If you want **experts in MXFP4 + attention in bf16** (gpt-oss-style), drop
`peft.backend` entirely and either:

- load the model from a checkpoint that already carries the right
  `quantization_config` (gpt-oss MXFP4 checkpoints, AWQ/GPTQ checkpoints), or
- set `quantize_moe_experts: true` and let axolotl quantize the expert
  tensors at load time while leaving attention in its checkpoint dtype.

Attention-only LoRA on a MoE model with quantized experts is the natural
training-time recipe; see [Expert Quantization](expert_quantization.qmd).

The equivalent bnb shorthand uses the same block:

```yaml
adapter: lora
peft:
  backend: bnb
  weight_dtype: nf4      # qlora + load_in_4bit
```

```yaml
adapter: lora
peft:
  backend: bnb
  weight_dtype: int8     # lora + load_in_8bit
```

Old-style configs (`adapter: qlora` + `load_in_4bit: true`) keep working and
default to the bnb backend.

## Constraints

- **DoRA** works with torchao (the kernels dequantize through the unified
  `dequantize_weight` helper). Other PEFT extras follow standard LoRA rules.
- **Merging adapters** with `axolotl merge-lora` requires
  `merge_method: legacy`. The memory-efficient merger simulates bnb's NF4
  quantization and does not yet understand torchao tensor subclasses.
- **PEFT's `TorchaoLoraLinear`** only supports INT8. Axolotl patches
  `dispatch_torchao` so INT4 / NF4 weights fall back to standard `Linear` LoRA
  layers (the kernels dequantize the base weight on access).
- `load_in_4bit` and `load_in_8bit` are bnb-only. They cannot be combined with
  `peft.backend: torchao` — the validator will reject the config.
- For weight-only quant at training time (`peft.backend: torchao`), MXFP4 is
  unavailable for dense linears — use `quantize_moe_experts: true` for MoE
  models. For inference-side quantization use the [QAT](qat.qmd) or
  [PTQ](quantize.qmd) flows.

## Example config

See [`examples/llama-3/qlora-torchao.yaml`](https://github.com/axolotl-ai-cloud/axolotl/blob/main/examples/llama-3/qlora-torchao.yaml).

## FSDP2

torchao tensor subclasses are first-class DTensor citizens, so the bnb-specific
FSDP2 patches (sharded-init shims, `Linear8bitLt` save patch) are skipped
automatically when `peft.backend: torchao` is in use.

```yaml
adapter: lora
peft:
  backend: torchao
  weight_dtype: nf4

fsdp_version: 2
fsdp_config:
  fsdp_offload_params: false
  fsdp_cpu_ram_efficient_loading: true
  fsdp_sharding_strategy: FULL_SHARD
  fsdp_state_dict_type: SHARDED_STATE_DICT
  fsdp_auto_wrap_policy: TRANSFORMER_BASED_WRAP
```
